name: Ethiopian Yirgacheffe
description: A classic Ethiopian Yirgacheffe with bright floral aromatics and a tea-like body. Delicate stone fruit sweetness with a clean, lingering finish.

name: Colombian Huila
description: Grown in the lush Huila department, this balanced medium roast offers a smooth, creamy body with gentle sweetness. A reliable everyday drinker.

name: Guatemalan Antigua
description: Sourced from volcanic soil in the Antigua valley. A rich, full-bodied cup with bittersweet chocolate depth and a smoky, nutty aftertaste.

name: Kenyan AA
description: Kenya's coveted AA grade, grown on the slopes of Mount Kenya. Intensely fruity with a wine-like complexity and a bold, bright acidity.

name: Sumatra Mandheling
description: Processed using the traditional wet-hull method, this Sumatran offers an earthy, full-bodied cup with low acidity and a syrupy, almost savory finish.

name: Costa Rican Tarrazú
description: Honey-processed in the high-altitude Tarrazú region. The mucilage left on the bean during drying imparts a natural sweetness and silky mouthfeel.

name: Panama Geisha
description: The legendary Geisha varietal from Boquete. Extraordinarily delicate with an almost tea-like transparency, explosive floral notes, and tropical fruit complexity.

name: Brazilian Cerrado
description: Grown on the vast Brazilian plateau and naturally processed in the sun. Low acidity with a heavy body and comforting nutty-chocolate sweetness. Ideal for espresso.

name: Yemen Mocha Matari
description: One of the world's oldest cultivated coffees. Grown on ancient terraced hillsides without irrigation. Wild, complex, and irreplaceable — a true heritage cup.

name: Rwanda Nyamasheke
description: From cooperatives near Lake Kivu, this Rwandan showcases the country's specialty potential. Vivid berry notes and a juicy acidity with a clean, sweet finish.

name: Mexican Chiapas
description: Organically grown by indigenous Tzotzil communities in the highlands of Chiapas. A mild, approachable cup with pleasant sweetness and a gentle nutty finish.

name: Papua New Guinea Sigri
description: From the Sigri estate in the Western Highlands. Complex and rustic with a distinctive earthy sweetness, full body, and an exotic spiced fruit character.

name: Ethiopian Sidama Natural
description: Naturally sun-dried on raised beds in Sidama. The natural process amplifies the fruit into an intensely jammy, dessert-like cup that coffee lovers adore.

name: Honduras Marcala
description: From the only region in Central America with a Denomination of Origin. Clean and balanced with a pleasant sweetness and soft, round acidity.

name: Tanzania Peaberry
description: Peaberry beans — a natural mutation where one oval bean forms instead of two — from the slopes of Kilimanjaro. Bright, lively, and more concentrated in flavor.

name: Peruvian Cajamarca
description: Certified organic from small farms in the northern Andes. A gentle, well-rounded cup with a light body and clean sweetness — great as a daily filter brew.

name: Burundi Kayanza
description: Burundi is quietly producing some of Africa's most exciting coffees. This Kayanza lot has a bright, complex acidity and a juicy red-fruit sweetness.

name: El Salvador Pacamara
description: The Pacamara is a large-bean hybrid unique to El Salvador. Honey-processed to enhance its natural sweetness, it delivers a delicate, dessert-like cup with exceptional clarity.

name: Indian Monsooned Malabar
description: Exposed to monsoon winds for weeks, swelling the beans and stripping acidity. The result is uniquely bold, low-acid, and savory — a divisive and fascinating cup.

name: House Blend
description: Our signature blend, crafted to work beautifully as espresso or filter. Combining Ethiopian brightness, Colombian balance, and Brazilian body into one harmonious cup.
